title: fix dependencies
body:
Clean up dependencies.

Signed-off-by: Mike Brown brownwm@us.ibm.com

comments.body: @mtrmac FYI I went ahead and got rid of the phony binary target as well. Is much cleaner just using the real binary `skopeo` as the target for all.

> @mtrmac FYI I went ahead and got rid of the phony binary target as well. Is much cleaner just using the real binary skopeo as the target for all.

Doesn’t this break the purpose of your PR?

> The failure case is make the skopeo binary.. touch a source file.. make the skopeo binary. Make fails to build the skopeo binary because it does not check the skopeo binary against the source tree. Required make clean in between builds.

Because `skopeo` is a non-phony target, `make skopeo` does not rebuild after source files are changed. And with #163 it is useful for `skopeo` to be non-phony, otherwise `make all && make install` would build it twice.

So, a non-phony `skopeo` dependency (for `make install` to fail without a build), and a phony `build` target (to force rebuilds) seems to work for most cases. It does not allow `make install` to automatically succeed in a new checkout without a `make {all,build}` first, but that is not truly essential and at least we have a reasonable error message now.

Sigh.  Suppose this is why folks fall to using .sh builds & installs. PITA but yes better to build every time than not, and it's ok to force the make all before the sudo make install, so.. here you go :-)
